---
title: "MIAC Baseball 2026 — Feature Engineering (Corrected)"
author: "Ben Trutwin"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = FALSE)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(tidyr)
library(janitor)
library(lubridate)
library(slider)
```

```{r Load-Data}
games_full <- read.csv("miac_games_full_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
team_pitching <- read.csv("team_pitching_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
team_fielding <- read.csv("team_fielding_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
individual_batting <- read.csv("individual_batting_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
individual_pitching <- read.csv("individual_pitching_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

games_full <- games_full %>% clean_names()

# Ensure date type
if (!inherits(games_full$game_date, "Date")) {
  games_full <- games_full %>% mutate(game_date = as.Date(game_date))
}

# Create unique game identifier
games_full <- games_full %>%
  mutate(game_id = row_number())

# Bug fix: the calendar scraper produces multi-line raw text that causes
# parse_team_score() to return NA for away_score/home_score.  The box-score
# scraper always populates away_runs/home_runs correctly, so use those as the
# authoritative source and fall back to the calendar-parsed values if present.
games_full <- games_full %>%
  mutate(
    home_score = as.integer(coalesce(home_runs, as.integer(home_score))),
    away_score = as.integer(coalesce(away_runs, as.integer(away_score)))
  )

# Remove games without scores (postponed/cancelled) — these will never have a target variable
games_full <- games_full %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_score) & !is.na(away_score))

cat("Rows after filtering to completed games:", nrow(games_full), "\n")

```

```{r Standardize-Teams}
standardize_team_name <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    str_replace_all("\\s*\\(MN\\)|\\s*\\(Wis\\.\\)|\\s*\\(IA\\)|\\s*\\(Ill\\.\\)", "") %>%
    str_replace_all("University", "") %>%
    str_replace_all("College", "") %>%
    str_replace_all("Univ\\.", "") %>%
    str_squish()
}

games_full <- games_full %>%
  mutate(
    home_team_std = standardize_team_name(home_team),
    away_team_std = standardize_team_name(away_team)
  )

team_pitching <- team_pitching %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(team_std = standardize_team_name(!!sym(names(.)[2])))

team_fielding <- team_fielding %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  mutate(team_std = standardize_team_name(!!sym(names(.)[2])))

```

```{r Rolling-Team-Features}
team_games <- games_full %>%
  select(game_id, game_date, home_team_std, away_team_std, home_score, away_score) %>%
  mutate(
    home_score = as.numeric(home_score),
    away_score = as.numeric(away_score)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(home_team_std, away_team_std),
    names_to = "home_away",
    values_to = "team_std"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    is_home = if_else(home_away == "home_team_std", 1L, 0L),
    team_score = if_else(is_home == 1L, home_score, away_score),
    opp_score  = if_else(is_home == 1L, away_score, home_score),
    win        = if_else(team_score > opp_score, 1L,
                         if_else(team_score < opp_score, 0L, NA_integer_)),
    run_diff   = team_score - opp_score
  ) %>%
  arrange(team_std, game_date)

team_games <- team_games %>%
  group_by(team_std) %>%
  arrange(game_date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    rf_last5  = slide_dbl(team_score, mean, .before = 5, .complete = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    ra_last5  = slide_dbl(opp_score,  mean, .before = 5, .complete = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    win_last5 = slide_dbl(win,        mean, .before = 5, .complete = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE),
    rd_last5  = slide_dbl(run_diff,   mean, .before = 5, .complete = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

```

```{r Join-Rolling}
home_feats <- team_games %>%
  filter(is_home == 1L) %>%
  select(game_id,
         home_rf_last5 = rf_last5,
         home_ra_last5 = ra_last5,
         home_win_last5 = win_last5,
         home_rd_last5 = rd_last5)

away_feats <- team_games %>%
  filter(is_home == 0L) %>%
  select(game_id,
         away_rf_last5 = rf_last5,
         away_ra_last5 = ra_last5,
         away_win_last5 = win_last5,
         away_rd_last5 = rd_last5)

games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(home_feats, by = "game_id") %>%
  left_join(away_feats, by = "game_id")

```

```{r Rest-Days}
rest_df <- team_games %>%
  group_by(team_std) %>%
  arrange(game_date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    prev_game_date = lag(game_date),
    rest_days = as.numeric(difftime(game_date, prev_game_date, units = "days"))
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

home_rest <- rest_df %>%
  filter(is_home == 1L) %>%
  select(game_id, home_rest_days = rest_days)

away_rest <- rest_df %>%
  filter(is_home == 0L) %>%
  select(game_id, away_rest_days = rest_days)

games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(home_rest, by = "game_id") %>%
  left_join(away_rest, by = "game_id")

```

```{r Rolling-SP-Features}
# Helper: convert baseball IP notation to decimal innings.
# X.1 = X + 1/3 innings, X.2 = X + 2/3 innings
ip_to_decimal <- function(ip) {
  x      <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(ip))
  whole  <- floor(x)
  thirds <- round((x - whole) * 10)   # extracts 0, 1, or 2
  whole + thirds / 3
}

# Build game-level SP IP in long format (one row per team per game)
sp_long <- games_full %>%
  select(game_id, game_date, home_team_std, away_team_std,
         home_starter_ip, away_starter_ip) %>%
  mutate(
    home_sp_ip_val = ip_to_decimal(home_starter_ip),
    away_sp_ip_val = ip_to_decimal(away_starter_ip)
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(home_team_std, away_team_std),
    names_to  = "side",
    values_to = "team_std"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    sp_ip = if_else(side == "home_team_std", home_sp_ip_val, away_sp_ip_val)
  ) %>%
  select(game_id, game_date, team_std, sp_ip) %>%
  arrange(team_std, game_date)

# Rolling 5-game average SP IP (matches .before = 5 convention used throughout)
sp_long <- sp_long %>%
  group_by(team_std) %>%
  arrange(game_date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    sp_ip_last5 = slide_dbl(sp_ip, mean, .before = 5, .complete = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Join back to games_full via game_id, filtering to home/away side
home_sp <- sp_long %>%
  inner_join(games_full %>% select(game_id, home_team_std),
             by = c("game_id", "team_std" = "home_team_std")) %>%
  select(game_id, home_sp_ip_last5 = sp_ip_last5)

away_sp <- sp_long %>%
  inner_join(games_full %>% select(game_id, away_team_std),
             by = c("game_id", "team_std" = "away_team_std")) %>%
  select(game_id, away_sp_ip_last5 = sp_ip_last5)

games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(home_sp, by = "game_id") %>%
  left_join(away_sp, by = "game_id")

```

```{r Rolling-Pitching-Proxy}
# Rolling ERA proxy: (runs_allowed * 9) / starter_ip_decimal per game,
# rolled over the last 5 games.
#
# Limitations vs true ERA:
#   1. Uses total runs allowed (not earned runs — unearned runs inflate this).
#   2. Denominator is starter IP, not full game IP, so it measures starter
#      workload rather than full bullpen workload.
#   3. WHIP, K/9, BB/9, OppBA cannot be computed at the game level because
#      per-game H, BB, K, and AB are not in the scraped box score data.
#      Season-level values (era_season, whip_season, etc.) remain the best
#      proxy for those stats.
#
# Guard: games where starter IP < 1/3 inning are excluded (NA) to avoid
# exploding ERA values from very short appearances.

# Build starter IP lookup in long format (one row per team per game)
starter_ip_lookup <- games_full %>%
  select(game_id, home_team_std, home_starter_ip,
                   away_team_std, away_starter_ip) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = c(home_team_std, away_team_std),
    names_to  = "side",
    values_to = "team_std"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    starter_ip_dec = ip_to_decimal(
      if_else(side == "home_team_std", home_starter_ip, away_starter_ip)
    )
  ) %>%
  select(game_id, team_std, starter_ip_dec)

# Attach starter IP and opp_score to team_games, then compute per-game ERA proxy
era_proxy_long <- team_games %>%
  select(game_id, game_date, team_std, opp_score) %>%
  left_join(starter_ip_lookup, by = c("game_id", "team_std")) %>%
  mutate(
    era_proxy = if_else(
      !is.na(starter_ip_dec) & starter_ip_dec >= (1/3),
      pmin(opp_score * 9 / starter_ip_dec, 27),  # cap at 27 (9 batters * 3 runs each)
      NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(team_std, game_date) %>%
  group_by(team_std) %>%
  arrange(game_date, .by_group = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    era_proxy_last5 = slide_dbl(era_proxy, mean, .before = 5, .complete = FALSE, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Join back as home/away columns via game_id
home_era_prx <- era_proxy_long %>%
  inner_join(games_full %>% select(game_id, home_team_std),
             by = c("game_id", "team_std" = "home_team_std")) %>%
  select(game_id, home_era_proxy_last5 = era_proxy_last5)

away_era_prx <- era_proxy_long %>%
  inner_join(games_full %>% select(game_id, away_team_std),
             by = c("game_id", "team_std" = "away_team_std")) %>%
  select(game_id, away_era_proxy_last5 = era_proxy_last5)

games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(home_era_prx, by = "game_id") %>%
  left_join(away_era_prx, by = "game_id")

cat("ERA proxy NA count (home):", sum(is.na(games_full$home_era_proxy_last5)), "\n")
cat("ERA proxy NA count (away):", sum(is.na(games_full$away_era_proxy_last5)), "\n")

```




```{r}
# Season-level team pitching quality from team_pitching_2026.csv.
# Per-game pitching breakdowns (WHIP, K/9, etc.) are not available in the
# box score data, so season totals serve as team quality indicators.
#
# The SO column is sometimes scraped as "K-HBP" combined (e.g. "174-44");
# we extract only the first number.  B/AVG values in the CSV can cause
# line-break parsing artifacts; we compute OppBA from H/AB instead.

pit_season <- team_pitching %>%
  # Drop scraping-artifact rows caused by B/AVG line breaks in the CSV
  filter(!is.na(suppressWarnings(as.numeric(era))),
         !is.na(team_std)) %>%
  distinct(team_std, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    ip_dec         = ip_to_decimal(ip),
    so_num         = as.numeric(str_extract(as.character(so), "^\\d+")),
    bb_num         = as.numeric(bb),
    h_num          = as.numeric(h),
    ab_num         = as.numeric(ab),
    era_season     = as.numeric(era),
    whip_season    = (bb_num + h_num) / ip_dec,
    k_per9_season  = (so_num * 9) / ip_dec,
    bb_per9_season = (bb_num * 9) / ip_dec,
    opp_ba_season  = h_num / ab_num
  ) %>%
  select(team_std, era_season, whip_season, k_per9_season, bb_per9_season, opp_ba_season)

home_pit <- pit_season %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("home_", .), .cols = -team_std) %>%
  rename(home_team_std = team_std)

away_pit <- pit_season %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("away_", .), .cols = -team_std) %>%
  rename(away_team_std = team_std)

games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(home_pit, by = "home_team_std") %>%
  left_join(away_pit, by = "away_team_std")

cat("Season pitching features joined. NA check:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(select(games_full, matches("era_season|whip|k_per9|bb_per9|opp_ba")))))
```


```{r Season-Fielding-Quality}
# Season-level team fielding quality from team_fielding_2026.csv.
# Fielding pct is computed from components (PO + A) / (PO + A + E) to avoid
# dependency on the column name that janitor::clean_names() assigns to "FLD%".

fld_season <- team_fielding %>%
  filter(!is.na(team_std)) %>%
  distinct(team_std, .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
  mutate(
    po_num  = as.numeric(po),
    a_num   = as.numeric(a),
    e_num   = as.numeric(e),
    dp_num  = as.numeric(dp),
    sba_num = as.numeric(sba),
    csb_num = as.numeric(csb),
    fld_pct_season = (po_num + a_num) / (po_num + a_num + e_num),
    errors_season  = e_num,
    dp_season      = dp_num,
    sba_season     = sba_num,
    csb_season     = csb_num
  ) %>%
  select(team_std, fld_pct_season, errors_season, dp_season, sba_season, csb_season)

home_fld <- fld_season %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("home_", .), .cols = -team_std) %>%
  rename(home_team_std = team_std)

away_fld <- fld_season %>%
  rename_with(~ paste0("away_", .), .cols = -team_std) %>%
  rename(away_team_std = team_std)

games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(home_fld, by = "home_team_std") %>%
  left_join(away_fld, by = "away_team_std")

cat("Season fielding features joined. NA check:\n")
print(colSums(is.na(select(games_full, matches("fld_pct|errors_season|dp_season|sba|csb")))))

```

```{r Pitcher-Features}
# Helper: strip W/L record and extract the last name from a starter string.
# Handles all formats found in box scores:
#   "Yotter, Teagan (W, 1-0)"  ->  "yotter"
#   "Ean McDaniel (L, 0-1)"    ->  "mcdaniel"
#   "S. Johnson"               ->  "johnson"
#   "Oliver Giel"              ->  "giel"
starter_last_name <- function(x) {
  x %>%
    # Remove win/loss/save records: (W, 1-0)  (L, 0-1)  (SV)  etc.
    str_replace_all("\\s*\\([WwLlSs][^)]*\\)", "") %>%
    str_squish() %>%
    # If "Last, First" format (comma present) take the part before the comma.
    # Otherwise take the LAST space-separated token (handles "First Last"
    # and "F. Last" initial formats).
    { ifelse(
        str_detect(., ","),
        str_trim(str_extract(., "^[^,]+")),
        word(., -1)
      )
    } %>%
    tolower()
}

# Parse individual pitching: extract team from "(Team Name)" and last name.
# Stats player format: "Yotter Teagan (Hamline)" or "Max Edwards (Saint John's)"
pitchers <- individual_pitching %>%
  clean_names() %>%
  rename(pitcher_raw = player, sp_era = era, sp_ip = ip) %>%
  mutate(
    # Pull the team name out of the trailing parenthetical
    pitcher_team_raw = str_extract(pitcher_raw, "(?<=\\()[^)]+(?=\\))"),
    pitcher_team_std = standardize_team_name(pitcher_team_raw),
    # Remove the "(Team)" portion and collapse whitespace
    name_clean       = str_squish(str_replace(pitcher_raw, "\\s*\\([^)]+\\)", "")),
    # The MIAC stats page most commonly lists last name first (e.g. "Yotter Teagan"),
    # so take the FIRST token as the matching key.
    pitcher_last     = tolower(word(name_clean, 1))
  )

# Add normalised last-name keys to games_full starters
games_full <- games_full %>%
  mutate(
    away_starter_std  = str_squish(away_starter),
    home_starter_std  = str_squish(home_starter),
    away_starter_last = starter_last_name(away_starter_std),
    home_starter_last = starter_last_name(home_starter_std)
  )

# Build per-side lookup tables keyed on (last_name, team_std)
away_pitch <- pitchers %>%
  select(pitcher_last, pitcher_team_std, sp_era, sp_ip) %>%
  rename(
    away_starter_last = pitcher_last,
    away_team_std     = pitcher_team_std,
    away_sp_era       = sp_era,
    away_sp_ip        = sp_ip
  )

home_pitch <- pitchers %>%
  select(pitcher_last, pitcher_team_std, sp_era, sp_ip) %>%
  rename(
    home_starter_last = pitcher_last,
    home_team_std     = pitcher_team_std,
    home_sp_era       = sp_era,
    home_sp_ip        = sp_ip
  )

# Join on last name + standardised team name to disambiguate same-surname pitchers
games_full <- games_full %>%
  left_join(away_pitch, by = c("away_starter_last", "away_team_std")) %>%
  left_join(home_pitch, by = c("home_starter_last", "home_team_std")) %>%
  # Only keep pitcher features when BOTH starters are matched.
  # (Only ~25 qualifiers exist in MIAC due to IP/G threshold.)
  mutate(
    has_both_pitchers = !is.na(away_sp_era) & !is.na(home_sp_era),
    away_sp_era = if_else(has_both_pitchers, away_sp_era, NA_real_),
    home_sp_era = if_else(has_both_pitchers, home_sp_era, NA_real_),
    away_sp_ip  = if_else(has_both_pitchers, away_sp_ip,  NA_real_),
    home_sp_ip  = if_else(has_both_pitchers, home_sp_ip,  NA_real_)
  )

```

```{r Context-Target}
games_full <- games_full %>%
  mutate(
    home_win = as.integer(home_score > away_score),
    home_field = 1L,
    is_conference = as.integer(is_conference),
    run_diff = as.numeric(home_score) - as.numeric(away_score)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(home_win))

cat("Modeling rows (games with a result):", nrow(games_full), "\n")

```
Get rid of primarily NA data. Lot of pitching statistics unfortunately are missing
```{r}
# Load your modeling data
df <- read.csv("miac_modeling_2026.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Drop columns with more than 80% NAs
na_threshold <- 0.8
cols_to_drop <- sapply(df, function(x) mean(is.na(x)) > na_threshold)
df_clean <- df[, !cols_to_drop]

# Optional: print dropped columns
cat("Dropped columns:\n")
print(names(df)[cols_to_drop])

# Save cleaned dataset
write.csv(df_clean, "miac_modeling_2026_clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```


```{r Save-Output}
write.csv(games_full, "miac_modeling_2026.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("Saved modeling dataset: miac_modeling_2026.csv\n")

```

```{r Diagnostics}
cat("Rows:", nrow(games_full), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(games_full), "\n\n")

# 1. Check for duplicated rows
cat("Duplicated rows:", sum(duplicated(games_full)), "\n\n")

# 2. Check NA patterns in key engineered features
na_summary <- games_full %>%
  select(
    # Rolling run features
    home_rf_last5, home_ra_last5, home_win_last5, home_rd_last5,
    away_rf_last5, away_ra_last5, away_win_last5, away_rd_last5,
    # Rest days
    home_rest_days, away_rest_days,
    # Rolling starter IP
    home_sp_ip_last5, away_sp_ip_last5,
    # Rolling ERA proxy (game-level, runs allowed per 9 starter innings)
    home_era_proxy_last5, away_era_proxy_last5,
    # Season pitching quality
    home_era_season, away_era_season,
    home_whip_season, away_whip_season,
    home_k_per9_season, away_k_per9_season,
    home_bb_per9_season, away_bb_per9_season,
    home_opp_ba_season, away_opp_ba_season,
    # Season fielding quality
    home_fld_pct_season, away_fld_pct_season,
    home_errors_season, away_errors_season,
    home_dp_season, away_dp_season,
    home_sba_season, away_sba_season,
    # Individual starter stats
    away_sp_era, home_sp_era, away_sp_ip, home_sp_ip
  ) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.))))

print(na_summary)

cat("\nPitcher feature rows with BOTH pitchers present:\n")
print(sum(games_full$has_both_pitchers))

cat("\nPitcher feature rows with missing pitchers:\n")
print(sum(!games_full$has_both_pitchers))

# 3. Check that rolling features change over time for a sample team
sample_team <- games_full$home_team_std[1]
cat("\nRolling feature preview for team:", sample_team, "\n")
games_full %>%
  filter(home_team_std == sample_team) %>%
  select(game_date, home_rf_last5, home_ra_last5, home_win_last5, home_rd_last5) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  print()

# 4. Check rest days for sanity
cat("\nRest day summary:\n")
summary(games_full$home_rest_days)
summary(games_full$away_rest_days)

# 5. Check target variable distribution
cat("\nHome win distribution:\n")
table(games_full$home_win)

# 6. Check for any columns that are all NA
all_na_cols <- names(which(colSums(!is.na(games_full)) == 0))
cat("\nColumns that are entirely NA:\n")
print(all_na_cols)

# 7. Check for any impossible values
cat("\nGames with negative rest days (should be 0):\n")
print(games_full %>% filter(home_rest_days < 0 | away_rest_days < 0))

cat("\nGames with impossible scores:\n")
print(games_full %>% filter(home_score < 0 | away_score < 0))

```
